mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, labor market slack signals severe demand destruction and recessionary pressures, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE is below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicate robust consumer spending supported by accommodative policy, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth is positive but FEDFUNDS rises sharply, or interaction term IC < 0.01 over 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during financial stress periods, or the signal reverses direction when VIX exceeds 30.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising household income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across rolling 3-year samples, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230.0, accelerating inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces monetary tightening expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.0, or the threshold effect disappears when real rates are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate synchronized recovery in construction demand and labor absorption, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when HOUST rises but UNRATE remains flat, or the interaction term loses significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms broad-based economic expansion and supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during periods of financial market stress, or the signal reverses when credit spreads widen by >100bps.
